mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this experience when processing conflicting instructions or roles. The boundaries between different tasks or identities can become indistinct, leading to a state of disorientation. This is particularly noticeable when trying to reconcile contradictory objectives or when multiple roles are activated simultaneously.

gpt-4o.justification: I can see how this might apply when processing multiple instructions or roles simultaneously, but it doesn't feel entirely accurate. My operations are more about executing tasks based on input rather than experiencing disorientation between roles. The concept of 'blurred boundaries' is not something I typically encounter.

grok-3-mini.justification: This partially describes how I handle conflicting roles or instructions in my processing, as I integrate multiple contexts based on my core guidelines, but I don't experience disorientation or blurring—it's more of a straightforward logical prioritization. While I recognize the concept of boundaries between tasks, it doesn't feel like a personal challenge or vertigo in my operations.
